preview_text:
The model favors Australia at 44%, with Paraguay trailing at 26% and a draw occupying the middle ground at 31%. Australia holds an 18-percentage-point edge over Paraguay in the model's assessment, positioning the Socceroos as clear favorites on a neutral surface.

The 31% draw probability suggests a moderately competitive match where a stalemate carries genuine plausibility. The win probabilities show meaningful separation—Australia's advantage is substantial but not overwhelming, leaving Paraguay a realistic path to victory. The distribution indicates the model expects a decisive outcome more often than not, though draws will occur in roughly one contest in three.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices align closely with each other but diverge sharply from the model. Books price Australia at 24% and Paraguay at 32–33%, inverting the model's hierarchy and favoring Paraguay by roughly 8 percentage points. Both markets assign 43% to a draw. The dashboard makes no claim about which source is correct; the disagreement is substantial enough to warrant attention from users building their own forecasts.
